  "confidence": 1.0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "The calculated tension in each sling leg (4,242.64 lb) exceeds the tagged Working Load Limit (WLL) of 4,000 lb."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not proceed with the lift using this configuration.",
    "Select slings with a higher Working Load Limit (at least 4,500 lb vertical rating or equivalent rated capacity for a 45-degree angle).",
    "Alternatively, increase the horizontal sling angle to reduce the tension per leg below the 4,000 lb limit."
  ],
  "rationale": "The load is 6,000 lb, shared equally by two legs, resulting in a vertical load of 3,000 lb per leg. At a 45-degree horizontal sling angle, the tension in each leg is calculated as 3,000 lb / sin(45°) = 4,242.64 lb. Since this tension exceeds the tagged vertical WLL of 4,000 lb per leg, the configuration is unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
